001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
022
023import java.io.*;
024import java.util.*;
025import java.util.function.*;
026import java.util.stream.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.commons.http.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.http.entity.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.http.header.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.http.part.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.http.remote.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.rest.client.remote.*;
037
038/**
039 * Next-generation transport-agnostic REST client.
040 *
041 * <p>
042 * Create instances via {@link #create()} or {@link #builder()}:
043 * <p class='bjava'>
044 *    <jc>// Minimal — uses the built-in JDK HttpClient transport</jc>
045 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>();
046 *
047 *    <jc>// Explicit transport (e.g. for tests)</jc>
048 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>builder</jsm>()
049 *       .transport(<jv>mockTransport</jv>)
050 *       .header(<js>"Accept"</js>, <js>"application/json"</js>)
051 *       .build();
052 * </p>
053 *
054 * <p>
055 * By default, {@code RestClient} uses {@link JavaHttpTransport} (backed by the JDK's
056 * {@link java.net.http.HttpClient}), which ships built into the {@code juneau-rest-client} artifact and
057 * requires no extra dependencies on Java 11+.  Pulling in one of the optional transport modules
058 * ({@code juneau-rest-client-apache-httpclient-45}, {@code -apache-httpclient-50}, {@code -okhttp},
059 * {@code -jetty}) registers a higher-priority provider via {@link java.util.ServiceLoader} that takes
060 * over automatically.  An explicit {@link Builder#transport(HttpTransport)} call always wins.
061 *
062 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
063 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
064 * </ul>
065 *
066 * @since 9.2.1
067 */
068@SuppressWarnings({
069   "resource" // transport is closed in RestClient.close(); this class owns it
070})
071public final class RestClient implements Closeable {
072
073   /**
074    * Default body converters applied in order when {@link RestRequest#body(Object)} is called.
075    *
076    * <p>
077    * Custom converters registered via {@link Builder#bodyConverter(BodyConverter[])} are prepended to this list.
078    * Call {@link Builder#bodyConverters(BodyConverter[])} to replace all defaults.
079    */
080   public static final List<BodyConverter<?>> DEFAULT_BODY_CONVERTERS = List.of(
081      BodyConverter.of(HttpBody.class, TransportBody::of),
082      BodyConverter.of(InputStream.class, is -> TransportBody.of(StreamBody.of(is))),
083      BodyConverter.of(byte[].class, bytes -> TransportBody.of(ByteArrayBody.of(bytes))),
084      BodyConverter.of(java.io.File.class, file -> TransportBody.of(FileBody.of(file)))
085   );
086
087   final HttpTransport transport;
088   final List<HttpHeader> defaultHeaders;
089   final List<HttpPart> defaultQueryData;
090   final String rootUrl;
091   final List<RestCallInterceptor> interceptors;
092   final RestLogger logger;
093   final List<BodyConverter<?>> bodyConverters;
094   final SerializerSet serializers;
095   final ParserSet parsers;
096   final Serializer defaultSerializer;
097   final Parser defaultParser;
098
099   private RestClient(Builder builder) {
100      this.transport = assertArgNotNull("transport",
101         builder.transport != null ? builder.transport : discoverTransport());
102      this.defaultHeaders = List.copyOf(builder.defaultHeaders);
103      this.defaultQueryData = List.copyOf(builder.defaultQueryData);
104      this.rootUrl = builder.rootUrl;
105      this.interceptors = List.copyOf(builder.interceptors);
106      this.logger = builder.logger;
107      this.bodyConverters = List.copyOf(builder.bodyConverters);
108      this.serializers = builder.serializers;
109      this.parsers = builder.parsers;
110      this.defaultSerializer = builder.defaultSerializer;
111      this.defaultParser = builder.defaultParser;
112   }
113
114   private static HttpTransport discoverTransport() {
115      var providers = new ArrayList<HttpTransportProvider>();
116      for (var p : ServiceLoader.load(HttpTransportProvider.class))
117         if (p.isAvailable())
118            providers.add(p);
119      if (providers.isEmpty())
120         return JavaHttpTransport.create();  // defensive fallback if META-INF/services was stripped (e.g. uber-jar shading)
121      providers.sort(Comparator.comparingInt(HttpTransportProvider::getPriority));
122      return providers.get(0).create();
123   }
124
125   /**
126    * Creates a new {@link RestClient} using auto-discovered transport.
127    *
128    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
129    */
130   public static RestClient create() {
131      return builder().build();
132   }
133
134   /** Returns a new {@link Builder}. */
135   public static Builder builder() {
136      return new Builder();
137   }
138
139   /**
140    * Creates a GET request to the given URL.
141    *
142    * @param url The target URL. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
143    * @return A new {@link RestRequest}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
144    */
145   public RestRequest get(String url) {
146      return request("GET", url);
147   }
148
149   /**
150    * Creates a POST request to the given URL.
151    *
152    * @param url The target URL. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
153    * @return A new {@link RestRequest}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
154    */
155   public RestRequest post(String url) {
156      return request("POST", url);
157   }
158
159   /**
160    * Creates a PUT request to the given URL.
161    *
162    * @param url The target URL. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
163    * @return A new {@link RestRequest}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
164    */
165   public RestRequest put(String url) {
166      return request("PUT", url);
167   }
168
169   /**
170    * Creates a PATCH request to the given URL.
171    *
172    * @param url The target URL. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
173    * @return A new {@link RestRequest}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
174    */
175   public RestRequest patch(String url) {
176      return request("PATCH", url);
177   }
178
179   /**
180    * Creates a DELETE request to the given URL.
181    *
182    * @param url The target URL. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
183    * @return A new {@link RestRequest}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
184    */
185   public RestRequest delete(String url) {
186      return request("DELETE", url);
187   }
188
189   /**
190    * Creates a HEAD request to the given URL.
191    *
192    * @param url The target URL. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
193    * @return A new {@link RestRequest}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
194    */
195   public RestRequest head(String url) {
196      return request("HEAD", url);
197   }
198
199   /**
200    * Creates a request with the given HTTP method and URL.
201    *
202    * @param method The HTTP method. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
203    * @param url The target URL. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
204    * @return A new {@link RestRequest}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
205    */
206   public RestRequest request(String method, String url) {
207      var resolvedUrl = rootUrl != null && !url.contains("://") ? rootUrl + url : url;
208      return new RestRequest(this, method, resolvedUrl);
209   }
210
211   /**
212    * Returns the underlying transport used by this client.
213    *
214    * @return The transport. Never <jk>null</jk>.
215    */
216   public HttpTransport getTransport() {
217      return transport;
218   }
219
220   /**
221    * Returns the explicitly-configured default serializer used for outbound bodies when the format is not otherwise
222    * discernable.
223    *
224    * <p>
225    * Only an explicit default set via {@link Builder#defaultSerializer(Serializer)} is honored — there is no implicit
226    * JSON fallback and no lone-registered-entry fallback.  A fully-unconfigured client returns {@link Optional#empty()}.
227    *
228    * <p>
229    * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
230    * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
231    * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
232    * (and possibly earlier).
233    *
234    * @return The configured default serializer, or {@link Optional#empty()} if none was set.
235    */
236   public Optional<Serializer> getDefaultSerializer() {
237      return opt(defaultSerializer);
238   }
239
240   /**
241    * Returns the parser matching the given response {@code Content-Type}, or the explicitly-configured default parser.
242    *
243    * <p>
244    * Resolution precedence: an exact media-type match against the client's {@link ParserSet} wins; otherwise the
245    * explicitly-configured {@link Builder#defaultParser(Parser) default parser} is used; otherwise
246    * {@link Optional#empty()} is returned.  There is no implicit JSON fallback and no lone-registered-entry fallback,
247    * so a fully-unconfigured client resolves to empty (callers throw <c>415 Unsupported Media Type</c>).
248    *
249    * <p>
250    * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
251    * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
252    * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
253    * (and possibly earlier).
254    *
255    * @param contentType The response {@code Content-Type} header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
256    * @return The matching parser, or {@link Optional#empty()} if nothing matched and no default parser is configured.
257    */
258   public Optional<Parser> getMatchingParser(String contentType) {
259      if (parsers != null && contentType != null) {
260         var p = parsers.getParser(contentType);
261         if (p.isPresent())
262            return p;
263      }
264      return opt(defaultParser);
265   }
266
267   /**
268    * Returns the registered request serializer matching the given media type, or {@link Optional#empty()} if none matches.
269    *
270    * <p>
271    * Unlike {@link #getDefaultSerializer()}, this method performs media-type-driven <i>selection</i> against the
272    * client's {@link SerializerSet} (via {@link SerializerSet#getSerializer(String)}) and returns
273    * {@link Optional#empty()} on no match so the caller can apply the locked no-match fallback (use the default
274    * serializer but still send the overridden {@code Content-Type} label).  An explicitly-configured single default
275    * serializer is also consulted when no set is registered.
276    *
277    * <p>
278    * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
279    * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
280    *
281    * @param mediaType The desired request media type. May be <jk>null</jk>/empty.
282    * @return The matching serializer, or {@link Optional#empty()} if no registered serializer matches.
283    */
284   public Optional<Serializer> getSerializerForMediaType(String mediaType) {
285      if (mediaType == null || mediaType.isEmpty() || serializers == null)
286         return opte();
287      return serializers.getSerializer(mediaType);
288   }
289
290   /**
291    * Returns the registered parser matching the given media type, or {@link Optional#empty()} if none matches.
292    *
293    * <p>
294    * Used by the next-gen {@code RemoteClient} as the {@code accept} fallback parser: it is consulted only when the
295    * response {@code Content-Type} matched no registered parser (or the response was unlabeled).  Returns
296    * {@link Optional#empty()} on no match so the caller can fall back to the default parser.
297    *
298    * <p>
299    * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
300    * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
301    *
302    * @param mediaType The desired parse media type. May be <jk>null</jk>/empty.
303    * @return The matching parser, or {@link Optional#empty()} if no registered parser matches.
304    */
305   public Optional<Parser> getParserForMediaType(String mediaType) {
306      if (mediaType == null || mediaType.isEmpty() || parsers == null)
307         return opte();
308      return parsers.getParser(mediaType);
309   }
310
311   /**
312    * Returns the default {@code Accept} header value advertising what this client can read.
313    *
314    * <p>
315    * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
316    * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
317    * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
318    * (and possibly earlier).
319    *
320    * @return The default {@code Accept} header value, or <jk>null</jk> if no parsers and no default parser are
321    *    configured (no implicit {@code application/json} fallback).
322    */
323   public String getDefaultAccept() {
324      if (parsers != null) {
325         var mts = parsers.getSupportedMediaTypes();
326         if (! mts.isEmpty())
327            return mts.stream().map(MediaType::toString).collect(Collectors.joining(", "));
328      }
329      if (defaultParser != null) {
330         var mts = defaultParser.getMediaTypes();
331         if (! mts.isEmpty())
332            return mts.stream().map(MediaType::toString).collect(Collectors.joining(", "));
333      }
334      return null;
335   }
336
337   /**
338    * Creates a Java proxy for the given {@link Remote}-annotated interface.
339    *
340    * <p>
341    * Each method call on the returned proxy will be translated into an HTTP request using this client.
342    *
343    * <p class='bjava'>
344    *    UserService <jv>svc</jv> = client.remote(UserService.<jk>class</jk>);
345    *    String <jv>user</jv> = <jv>svc</jv>.getUser(<js>"42"</js>);
346    * </p>
347    *
348    * @param <T> The interface type.
349    * @param iface The interface class. Must be annotated with {@link Remote}. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
350    * @return A proxy instance backed by this client. Never <jk>null</jk>.
351    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code iface} is not an interface or not annotated with {@code @Remote}.
352    */
353   public <T> T remote(Class<T> iface) {
354      return new RemoteClient(this).create(iface);
355   }
356
357   @Override /* Closeable */
358   public void close() throws IOException {
359      transport.close();
360   }
361
362   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
363   // Builder
364   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
365
366   /**
367    * Fluent builder for {@link RestClient}.
368    *
369    * <p>
370    * <b>Beta — API subject to change.</b>
371    *
372    * @since 9.2.1
373    */
374   public static final class Builder {
375
376      HttpTransport transport;
377      final List<HttpHeader> defaultHeaders = new ArrayList<>();
378      final List<HttpPart> defaultQueryData = new ArrayList<>();
379      String rootUrl;
380      final List<RestCallInterceptor> interceptors = new ArrayList<>();
381      RestLogger logger;
382      List<BodyConverter<?>> bodyConverters = new ArrayList<>(DEFAULT_BODY_CONVERTERS);
383      SerializerSet serializers;
384      ParserSet parsers;
385      Serializer defaultSerializer;
386      Parser defaultParser;
387      final List<Serializer> serializerList = new ArrayList<>();
388      final List<Parser> parserList = new ArrayList<>();
389
390      private Builder() {}
391
392      /**
393       * Sets the HTTP transport to use.
394       *
395       * <p>
396       * If not set, {@link RestClient} discovers a transport via {@link java.util.ServiceLoader}.  When no
397       * sibling transport module is on the classpath, the built-in {@link JavaHttpTransport} (backed by the JDK's
398       * {@link java.net.http.HttpClient}) is used as the default.
399       *
400       * @param value The transport. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
401       * @return This object.
402       */
403      public Builder transport(HttpTransport value) {
404         transport = value;
405         return this;
406      }
407
408      /**
409       * Sets a root URL prefix applied to all relative request URLs (those without {@code ://}).
410       *
411       * @param value The root URL (e.g. {@code "https://api.example.com/v1"}). May be <jk>null</jk>.
412       * @return This object.
413       */
414      public Builder rootUrl(String value) {
415         rootUrl = value;
416         return this;
417      }
418
419      /**
420       * Adds default headers sent with every request.
421       *
422       * @param value The headers to add. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
423       * @return This object.
424       */
425      public Builder headers(HttpHeader... value) {
426         defaultHeaders.addAll(Arrays.asList(value));
427         return this;
428      }
429
430      /**
431       * Adds a default header sent with every request.
432       *
433       * @param name The header name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
434       * @param value The header value (eager). May be <jk>null</jk>.
435       * @return This object.
436       */
437      public Builder header(String name, String value) {
438         defaultHeaders.add(HttpHeaderBean.of(name, value));
439         return this;
440      }
441
442      /**
443       * Adds a default header with a lazy value, evaluated at each request.
444       *
445       * @param name The header name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
446       * @param value Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
447       * @return This object.
448       */
449      public Builder header(String name, Supplier<String> value) {
450         defaultHeaders.add(HttpHeaderBean.of(name, value));
451         return this;
452      }
453
454      /**
455       * Adds default query parameters appended to every request URL.
456       *
457       * @param value The parts to add. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
458       * @return This object.
459       */
460      public Builder queryData(HttpPart... value) {
461         defaultQueryData.addAll(Arrays.asList(value));
462         return this;
463      }
464
465      /**
466       * Adds a default query parameter appended to every request URL.
467       *
468       * @param name The parameter name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
469       * @param value The parameter value (eager). May be <jk>null</jk>.
470       * @return This object.
471       */
472      public Builder queryData(String name, String value) {
473         defaultQueryData.add(HttpPartBean.of(name, value));
474         return this;
475      }
476
477      /**
478       * Adds one or more lifecycle interceptors called before/after each request.
479       *
480       * @param value The interceptors to add. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
481       * @return This object.
482       */
483      public Builder interceptors(RestCallInterceptor... value) {
484         interceptors.addAll(Arrays.asList(value));
485         return this;
486      }
487
488      /**
489       * Sets the logger called at the end of every request (success or failure).
490       *
491       * @param value The logger. May be <jk>null</jk> to disable logging.
492       * @return This object.
493       */
494      public Builder logger(RestLogger value) {
495         logger = value;
496         return this;
497      }
498
499      /**
500       * Prepends custom body converters to the default converter list.
501       *
502       * <p>
503       * Custom converters are checked before the defaults when {@link RestRequest#body(Object)} is called.
504       *
505       * @param value The converters to prepend. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
506       * @return This object.
507       */
508      public Builder bodyConverter(BodyConverter<?>... value) {
509         var prepended = new ArrayList<BodyConverter<?>>(Arrays.asList(value));
510         prepended.addAll(bodyConverters);
511         bodyConverters = prepended;
512         return this;
513      }
514
515      /**
516       * Replaces the entire body converter list (including defaults).
517       *
518       * <p>
519       * Use this when you want full control over body conversion, including disabling the built-in defaults.
520       *
521       * @param value The complete replacement converter list. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
522       * @return This object.
523       */
524      public Builder bodyConverters(BodyConverter<?>... value) {
525         bodyConverters = list(value);
526         return this;
527      }
528
529      /**
530       * Sets the serializer registry used for outbound bodies.
531       *
532       * @param value The serializer set. May be <jk>null</jk>.
533       * @return This object.
534       */
535      public Builder serializers(SerializerSet value) {
536         serializers = value;
537         return this;
538      }
539
540      /**
541       * Sets the parser registry used for inbound bodies.
542       *
543       * @param value The parser set. May be <jk>null</jk>.
544       * @return This object.
545       */
546      public Builder parsers(ParserSet value) {
547         parsers = value;
548         return this;
549      }
550
551      /**
552       * Appends serializers (built into a {@link SerializerSet} at build time if no set was supplied).
553       *
554       * @param value The serializers to append. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
555       * @return This object.
556       */
557      public Builder serializer(Serializer... value) {
558         serializerList.addAll(Arrays.asList(value));
559         return this;
560      }
561
562      /**
563       * Appends parsers (built into a {@link ParserSet} at build time if no set was supplied).
564       *
565       * @param value The parsers to append. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
566       * @return This object.
567       */
568      public Builder parser(Parser... value) {
569         parserList.addAll(Arrays.asList(value));
570         return this;
571      }
572
573      /**
574       * Designates the default serializer used for outbound bodies when no registered serializer matches the
575       * requested media type.
576       *
577       * <p>
578       * This is the explicit, opt-in way to restore a fallback serializer.  Without it, a request that cannot be
579       * matched to a registered serializer (and any body requiring serialization on a serializer-less client) fails
580       * rather than silently defaulting to JSON.  Set {@code defaultSerializer(JsonSerializer.DEFAULT)} to recover
581       * the pre-10.0 implicit-JSON behavior.
582       *
583       * @param value The default serializer. May be <jk>null</jk>.
584       * @return This object.
585       */
586      public Builder defaultSerializer(Serializer value) {
587         defaultSerializer = value;
588         return this;
589      }
590
591      /**
592       * Designates the default parser used when the response {@code Content-Type} is absent or matches no registered
593       * parser.
594       *
595       * <p>
596       * This is the explicit, opt-in way to restore a fallback parser.  Without it, a response whose
597       * {@code Content-Type} matches no registered parser fails with <c>415 Unsupported Media Type</c> rather than
598       * silently parsing as JSON.  Set {@code defaultParser(JsonParser.DEFAULT)} to recover the pre-10.0
599       * implicit-JSON behavior.
600       *
601       * @param value The default parser. May be <jk>null</jk>.
602       * @return This object.
603       */
604      public Builder defaultParser(Parser value) {
605         defaultParser = value;
606         return this;
607      }
608
609      /**
610       * Builds and returns the {@link RestClient}.
611       *
612       * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
613       */
614      public RestClient build() {
615         if (serializers == null && ! serializerList.isEmpty())
616            serializers = SerializerSet.create().add(serializerList.toArray(new Serializer[0])).build();
617         if (parsers == null && ! parserList.isEmpty())
618            parsers = ParserSet.create().add(parserList.toArray(new Parser[0])).build();
619         return new RestClient(this);
620      }
621   }
622}
